column15:
Institution Profile: Fawohoyeden Technical Institute

Fawohoyeden Technical Institute (FTI) is a vital educational hub located in the Asunafo North Municipality of Ghana’s Ahafo Region. Since its inception, the institute has been dedicated to providing quality Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) to the youth, empowering them with the practical skills essential for industrialization and sustainable economic growth.

Vision Statement
To be a premier institution of excellence in technical and vocational training, producing innovative, highly skilled, and globally competitive professionals for the modern industrial workforce.

Mission Statement
To provide high-quality, competency-based technical training through advanced pedagogical methods, while fostering a culture of discipline, entrepreneurship, and integrity to ensure the holistic development of students for national progress.

Infrastructure and Operational Challenges
Despite its critical role in human resource development, Fawohoyeden Technical Institute faces significant operational hurdles that impede its ability to deliver on its mandate. Foremost among these is the inadequate number of classrooms. The current facilities are overstretched by increasing student enrollment, resulting in overcrowded learning environments that limit effective student-teacher interaction.

Furthermore, the lack of sufficient dormitories for both boys and girls remains a major concern. This deficiency forces a large number of students to seek unregulated accommodation in the surrounding community, which poses safety risks and undermines the school's disciplinary framework. The institute also lacks a functional dining hall, compelling students to eat in improvised or outdoor settings, which compromises hygiene and communal bonding. Similarly, the absence of a dedicated assembly hall means that all major school functions, including examinations and official gatherings, are often held outdoors and are subject to weather disruptions.

Beyond physical structures, the institute grapples with an inadequate staffing issue. A shortage of specialized technical instructors and support personnel has created a high student-to-teacher ratio, making it difficult to provide the personalized mentorship required for technical mastery. Addressing these gaps is crucial for FTI to fulfill its potential.

column14: OITI is a leading Technical Institute located in Effia - Kwesimintsim  Municipal (Takoradi - Ghana). As a mixed day second cycle technical institute, OITI focuses on practical, hands - on skills acquisition in various technical, vocational, and hospitality fields. It equips students with industry-relevant competencies to support career development.
column15:
OITI operates as a leading Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). It specializes in practical, hands-on skills acquisition to empower students — particularly youth — with industry-relevant competencies. 
Its guiding motto is “Together we help ourselves,” reflecting a strong emphasis on self-empowerment, community support, entrepreneurship, and real-world application of skills to meet Ghana’s growing human resource demands.
Location and Status

Location: Effia, Effia - Kwesimitsim Municipal, Takoradi, Western Region.
Category: Category C Technical Institute.
Gender: Mixed (boys and girls).
Residential Status: Day school (non-boarding).
Affiliation: Recognized under Ghana’s TVET Service framework.

Academic and Training Focus
OITI offers practical programs in various technical, vocational, and hospitality fields. While specific course lists vary, the institute is known for departments including:

Fashion Designing Technology
Catering and Hospitality Management (with workplace experience learning components)
Building Construction Technology
Architectural Drafting
General Textiles
Electrical Engineering Technology
Welding and Fabrication Technology
Business Secretarial 

The curriculum emphasizes hands-on training, industry partnerships, digital skills introduction, and entrepreneurship. Students participate in real-world activities such as fashion and modelling shows, musical productions, SRC week events, and industrial attachments.

Principal: Mr. Emmanuel Sagoe
The institute actively collaborates with corporate partners (e.g., Tullow Ghana Corps National Foundation, WUSC - Ghana), and TVET stakeholders. It has hosted visits from industry leaders and received support for infrastructure and learning materials.
OITI promotes inclusivity, with notable participation of physically challenged students in school events.

OITI boasts a vibrant student community with activities including:
SRC Week celebrations
Fashion and modelling shows
Sports, cultural events, and skills competitions
Workplace experience learning (industrial attachments)

The Institution positions itself as a dynamic environment where students develop both technical expertise and soft skills for career success.
Broader Context
OITI forms part of the GTVET Schools with focus remaining on bridging the skills gap, reducing unemployment, and supporting national development through quality TVET delivery.
For the most current details on specific programs, admission requirements, or fees, it is recommended to contact the institute directly via its official Facebook page (@OITI Effia - Kwesimintsim) or Tiktok (@ OITI Effia - Kwesimintsim)
OITI continues to play a vital role in Ghana’s TVET landscape by producing skilled, confident graduates ready to contribute to the economy.

column14: Manso Amenfi Technical Institute, established October 29, 2021 in Wassa Amenfi Central District, Ghana, offers technical education to 721 students with 26 staff. Equipped with modern facilities, ICT labs, and dormitories, it continues a legacy of vocational excellence training youth.
column15:
Manso Amenfi Technical Institute is in the Western Region of Ghana at Wassa Amenfi Central District, off the Manso Amenfi - Asankragwa highway. It was established on the 29th of October 2021. It is situated on 13.92 acres of land.
Manso Amenfi Technical Institute currently has a teaching staff strength of 19 of which 12 are males and 7 are females.
Non-teaching staff are 7, 4 females and 3 males
We have a total student’s population of 721. Form one comprises of 183 boys and 77 girls, form two has 143 boys and 65 girls and form three has 174 boys and 79 girls.
The Institute can boast of the following facilities: an administration block which has an office for the Principal, Assistant Principals, Accounts office, Secretary’s office, library, staff common room, kitchen and washrooms.
There is also a 100 capacity two storey dormitory facility for both boys and girls with modern beds and mattresses. The Institute can also boast of a 11unit classroom block with furniture, an ultra-modern ICT lab well equipped with laptops and desktops.
A well-equipped workshop with modern tools and equipment for all the departments. The Institute has a 33-seater brand new Yutong bus. 

HISTORY
Manso Amenfi Technical Institute (MANSTECH) which was formerly Manso Amenfi Vocational Training Institute affectionately called MAVOTRAIN was established on the 29th of October 2021 by Rev. Moses Aidoo the then Centre Manager for Kumasi Vocational a Training Institute (KVTI) and a native of Manso Amenfi. He was supported by the chief of Manso Amenfi, Nana Amoabeng Ababio II, the then Member of Parliament Mr. John Abu, Nana Attah Esuah Unit Committee Chairman, Management of SMS Timber Company and the Amenfi West District Assembly. It was later adopted by the National Vocational Training Institute (NVTI) on January 1st, 2003.
Manso Amenfi Vocational Training Institute Started in the then Bamboo Factory building with four trades namely, Dressmaking, Catering, Building Construction and General Electricals. Mr Bernard Baidoo Acted as manager before NVTI posted its first Manager Mr. Erasmus Sam.
Mr. Erasmus Sam the first manager of the Institute after the adoption by NVTI created the Motor Vehicle Mechanics Department making the number of trades to be five

Manso Amenfi Vocational Training Institute has trained more than 1000 youth in Wassa Amenfi

column14:
The Accra Technical Training Centre (ATTC) is a vocational / technical co-educational institution located at Kokomlemle in Accra.

The ATTC was conceived as a joint project by the Governments of Ghana and Canada “… To provide theoretical and practical instruction for the occupations as may be required from time to time”. Thus, from its cradle it was conceived as training institution to train skilled manpower for Ghana’s industrial requirements, and it marked the first major Canadian support to Ghana in the field of technical education and training.
column15:
The beginnings of the Accra Technical Training Centre dates back to the early 1960’s when one Mr. Arnold Doyle, the Director of Vocational and Technical Education or the Canadian Province of Saskatchewan conducted a feasibility study for the establishment of a technical school in Ghana by the then Canadian External Aid Office, now Canadian International Development Agency (CIDA). Mr. Doyle became the Canadian Co-ordinator for the project when the Canadian Government commissioned the Canadian Province of Saskatchewan to implement the ATTC project

The Ghanaian counterpart to Mr. Doyle was Mr. Frank Hutchful who was then Ghana’s Director of Apprenticeship and Duty Chief Technical Education Officer.

Under the aegis of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Governments of Ghana and Canada, construction of the Centre commenced in 1964. The External Aid Department provided architectural services and all constructional materials. In addition, nearly all the instructional equipment and furniture and the initial consumable training materials were provided under the Agreement.

The Government of Ghana provided the site for the Centre, Courtesy of the Ga traditional Council. Building materials that could be purchased locally, labour and paid all shipping costs. Additionally, all operating costs (recurrent expenditure), maintenance costs and the salaries of Ghanaian staff were borne by the Government of Ghana.

Meanwhile, in accordance with the terms of the Agreement, a substantial number of Ghanaian technicians were sent to Canada for advance training in vocational and technical education and instructor training in Saskatchewan. The programme was to serve a dual purpose, viz, to enhance their professional skills and efficiency and to prepare them for an eventual takeover of the administration of the Centre.

Construction of the main buildings was completed in 1965. An initial team of 14 Canadian Technical Advisors all from Saskatchewan and under the able leadership of one Mr. George Brown as the fist Canadian Principal for the Centre arrived in Ghana during the latter part of 1965 and early 1966 to finish the construction and equipping of the Centre together with the first batch of six Ghanaian Instructors who had by then returned from Canada and started the operation of the new Centre in July, 1966.

Formal opening of the Centre in July, 1966 as performed by Mr. Donald S. Macdonald, then Canadian Minister for External Affairs representing the Canadian Government and Mr. A.K. Deku, the then Commissioner for Education in the national Liberation Council (NLC) Government, representing the Government of Ghana.

column14: Amoafo Technical Institute has a clear vision and mission focused on promoting technical and vocational skills for national development. we foster academic excellence, moral values, and personal development.  our core values emphasize excellence, teamwork, respect, and integrity
column15:
Amoafo Technical Institute is a government Institution located in Ashanti-Bekwai, precisely Amoafo. The School came to existence as a result of mainstream schools being attached to the Department of Social Welfare Rehabilitation centers and other adjuncts.  

In the year 2002, under the able leadership of Mr. Fred Sakyi Boafo (RTD), the then District Director of Social Welfare, and the Deputy Director in charge of Family Unit of the Department of Social Welfare Head Office, Accra, Amoafo Technical Institute was established to be the mainstream School for the Department of Social Welfare in Amoafo. The year under review marks the twenty-fourth (24th) year of the school’s existence from August, 2002 to date.  

The School currently works directly under Ghana TVET Service after government’s confiscation of certain school which Amoafo Technical Institute was privileged to be part of.
 
VISION
To promote Technical and Vocational skill that can make a distinctive contribution to national development as an institution of academic and technical excellence whose graduates are endowed with real practical ability and moral vision of life in all spheres of life.
OUR MISSION STATEMENT
The mission of the school is to promote academic excellence, moral values as well as the integral personal development of it’s students and their commitment to serve.
    Our Values 
•	Pursuit of Excellence
•	Teamwork
•	Respect for all 
•	Truth, honesty and integrity
CORE MANDATE
Amoafo Technical Institute is dedicated to providing high-quality Technical Education, Training and skills development that prepares students for successful careers and contributes to the socio-economic development of Ghana.

OBJECTIVE: 
 The prime objectives of the Institute are to: 
	Implement approved national policies and programmes relating to pre-tertiary technical and vocational education and training.
	Ensure 98% retention of learners at all levels.
	Obtain 100% pass rate for all examinations.
	Ensure students are well equipped with the adequate skills to fit in for the ready job market.

  PROGRAMMES 
The institution offers ten (10) programmes 
1)	Building Construction Technology
2)	Wood Construction Technology
3)	Fashion Design Technology
4)	Hospitality & Catering Management
5)	Welding and Fabrication Technology
6)	Electrical Engineering Technology
7)	Plumbing & Gas Fitting Technology
8)	Cosmetology & Hair Technology
9)	Automobile Engineering Technology 
10)	Computer hardware Technology

vocational training centre was established in the year 1960 by the statutory 
enablement of the criminal justice act, to provide technical training to 
student.  
By the promulgation of the Pre-tertiary Act,2020 Act 1049, the school has 
been realigned from the Social Welfare Department to the GHANA 
TECHNICAL VOCATIONAL EDUCATION AND TRAINING SERVICE 
(GTVET SERVICE) of the Ministry of Education. The realignment 
culminated to the name of the school being changed from Social Welfare 
Technical and Vocational Training Centre to ESSIPUN TECHNICAL 
INSTITUTE to suit our new status of a fully-fledged technical institute.  
With the birth and realignment with GTVET service, Essipun technical 
institute is the new wave of technical education.

column14:
KIBI TECHNICAL INSTITUTE
A BRIEF HISTORY ABOUT THE SCHOOL
Kibi Technical Institute – was established on the 1st September, 1978 with only two (2) department.  They were masonry and, carpentry, and joinery with twenty (20) trainees to become master craftmen within three (3) years.
As the institute advanced, other programmes such as Automobile Engineering Technology, Electrical and Electronics Technology Engineering, Leather works, Plumbing and Gas fitting and Computer Hardware were introduced and maintained till date.
These programmes came to light due to the increase demand of skills from industries, organization and self development.
To ensure gender equality and female empowerment, early in the years 2000 female trainees were also admitted to embark on some male dominated programmes and since then the institute has been admitting both male and female for all programmes. 
The examining body over the years was NVTI.
Past trainees of the institute have received training from major stakeholders both foreign partners and local partners as well.
The Institute has also introduced new programmes such as Garment and Fashion and welding and fabrication to meet the standards facilitation, training and assessing, staff of the institute went through intensive CBT training programme in 2012, and in 2017.  The trainees enrolment has been increasing over the admission years.
The institute has also recorded some training programmes for some government special initiative programmes such as Gender Responsive skills and Community Development Project (GRSCDP) from 2011 to 2013 and Alternative Livelihood Project (ALP) from August, 2018 to July 2019.
Currently, the institutes take its examination from NVTI, NABPTEX and GES Cert II depending on programme of study in various levels – Prof I, II, NVTI Cert I, GES TEU Cert II and NABPTEX core.
Employment history of our past trainees have always been positive, professional and successful without any report of incompetence, deviance, and other unprofessional job ethics. 
3.0	VISION
To train high quality personnel for self-employment within the working industry.

4.0	MISSION
The Institute exists to ensure improvement in the living standards of people by giving them opportunities in the working industry

column14: St. Mary's Technical Institute Donkorkrom-Adeemmra is the only all female Technical/Vocational Institution in the Kwahu Afram Plains North of the Eastern Region of Ghana. The school was established in 1999 as a private Institution but later absorbed by the Government of Ghana in 2010 as a public institution. The school offers 9 programmes namely; Fashion, Catering, Textiles Technology, Wood Technology, Welding and Fabrication, Fine Art Technology, Business ICT, Business Accounting and Business Secretarial.
column15: St. Marys Technical Institute Donkorkrom -Adeemmra was established with the aim of equipping young girls and women with required skills in Catering, Hairdressing and Fashion Designing to become self-employed and to earn a living. It was established as a private Institute in 1999 and was absorbed by the Government of Ghana in the year 2010. The institution has put in place well structured environment to equip many people in various trade areas such as Fashion and Catering through competency- based Training ( CBT). The institution is situated in the Kwahu Afram Plains North, specifically, Adeemmra Kwahu, a Town near the District Capital - Donkorkrom. The school can be reached  outside the Plains through either Kpando- Tokor in the Volta Region or Kwahu - Adawso in the Eastern Region by boat or ferry. The need to establish a Vocational and Technical Institute in a deprived area like the Kwahu North ( Afram Plains) district could not be overlooked by the pioneer missionaries of the society of the Divine Word, SVD, a religious society made up of priest ( Reverend Fathers) and lay brothers ( Reverend Brothers) within the catholic church, who came to evangelize the inhabitants of this remote area in the early 1940's. The idea became a reality during a parish council meeting at Abomasarefo in 1998, under the auspices of Rev. Fr. Joseph Pitchler, SVD, the then Parish priest of St. Marys Parish, Adeemmra, a resolution was passed and a committe was set under the chairmanship of  Mr. Aikins George, a teacher and assistant catechist of Adeemmra, to prepare the modalities for the birth of this noble institution. The institution was temporarily offered some classrooms at the JHS and through the benevolence of the Kwahu Afram Plains District Assembly, an unused building was donated  to the Institute to be used as a hostel now a boarding house. Later, the Catholic Diocese of Koforidua acquired a parcel of land for the Construction of permanent structures which started in 2003 with funds from overseas donors. Currently, the Institute has moved to its permanent site. The school was officially opened on lst March 1999 by Rev Fr. Paul Pio, SVD who succeeded Fr. Joseph Pitchler with a student population of 15 and a moderate staff of three namely; Mary Asantewaa, Hairdressing tutor, Rita Lino, Fashion tutor and Lino Esther, the acting Principal. Miss Lino Esther was succeeded by Rev. Sr. Angelina Gerhaz as acting Principal from 2005 to 2009. Rev Sr. Lamisi Adomolgo, SSpS took over as Principal until the school was absorbed by the Government of Ghana in 2010 and since operated as a day and boarding Institute approved by GES. The current Principal, Rev. Sr. Elizabeth Biga SSpS, took over from Sr. Lamisi as Principal from 2013 to date.  The school currently runs 9 programmes. The district Director of Education for Kwahu Afram Plains North used to oversee the Administration of the school, that is prior to December 2022, however, with the advent of Ghana TVET Service which came into effect from December 2022, the Eastern Regional Director of Ghana TVET Service oversees the Administration of the school on behalf of the Director General of Ghana TVET Service.

column14: Suhum Technical Institute, established in 1964 by Mr. J. C. Amoah, began as a Rural Training Centre for women. It evolved through several name changes, expanding programmes to include technical and vocational courses. Today, under Ghana’s TVET reforms, it provides skills training for youth and contributes significantly to workforce development.
column15:
Profile of Suhum Technical Institute
Suhum Technical Institute is a government-owned Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Suhum in the Eastern Region of Ghana, adjacent to Suhum Presbyterian Senior High School. The Institute is committed to providing quality technical and vocational education aimed at equipping young people with practical, employable, and entrepreneurial skills for national development.
Established in 1964 under the leadership of Mr. J. C. Amoah, the Institute has undergone significant transformation over the years in response to changing educational and labour market demands. From its beginnings as a Rural Training Centre focused on skills training for women, it has evolved into a modern technical institute offering structured programmes for both male and female students. Today, it operates under Ghana’s TVET framework, emphasizing Competency-Based Training (CBT) to ensure graduates are industry-ready.
The Institute offers a wide range of programmes, including Cookery, Dressmaking, Hairdressing, Secretarialship, Computer Science, Electrical Installation, Plumbing, and Building and Construction. These programmes are designed to provide both theoretical knowledge and hands-on practical experience, preparing students for employment, self-employment, and further education.
Suhum Technical Institute is equipped with essential training facilities, including workshops, classrooms, and an ICT laboratory that supports both teaching and income-generating activities. The school environment is spacious and conducive to learning, enabling the effective delivery of both short-term and long-term training programmes.
The Institute maintains strong linkages with industry and stakeholders to ensure its training remains relevant to current market needs. Through partnerships, industrial attachments, and practical exposure, students gain valuable experience that enhances their employability.
Guided by its vision to produce skilled and competent manpower for industry, the Institute upholds core values such as excellence, discipline, integrity, innovation, teamwork, and lifelong learning. These values shape both academic delivery and student character development.
With nearly six decades of continuous service, Suhum Technical Institute has made significant contributions to human capital development in Ghana. It remains committed to expanding access, improving quality, and strengthening its role as a leading TVET institution dedicated to skills development and national growth.

column14: Techiman Technical Institute is a reputable TVET institution committed to providing quality technical and vocational education. The institute equips students with practical skills, innovation, and entrepreneurship competencies to meet industry demands, foster self-reliance, and contribute meaningfully to national development through skilled workforce training.
column15:
Techiman Technical Institute formerly known as the Technology Solutions Center (TSC) is located in the Techiman Metropolitan area of the Bono East Region, Ghana. It was established in early 2022 to provide Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). The institute was founded with the mission of equipping students with practical, hands-on skills to enhance their employability and contribute significantly to the local economy of Techiman and its environs.
Development and Growth
In its formative years, Techiman Technical Institute began with two core programs:
1. Welding and Fabrication Technology
2. Mechanical Engineering Technology
As the demand for skilled labor increased in the region, the institute expanded its curriculum to include additional programs:
Garments and Fashion Technology, Electrical Engineering Technology and Building Construction. The growth of these programs was made possible through the dedication and hard work of the institute’s staff and volunteers, who were instrumental in developing the school’s reputation and capabilities.
Expansion and Changes
Mr. Maxwell Ntiamoah was the first appointed principal of the institute, with an initial staff strength of five and a modest student population of sixty- one (61), comprised of Form 2 and Form 3 students. Under his leadership, the foundation of the institute was laid.
Following the exit of Mr. Ntiamoah, Mr. Alfred Boham was appointed as the substantive principal in September 2024. Under his leadership, the institute has seen remarkable growth. The staff strength has increased to twenty-four (24), with a current student population of Eight hundred and twenty-three (823), supported by nine (9) volunteers.
The institute continues to operate out of a 6-unit classroom block built by the Techiman Metropolitan Assembly with funding from GETFund. This facility has been critical in supporting the institute’s operations and accommodating its growing population.
Techiman Technical Institute remains committed to its vision of providing quality Technical Education and contributing to the Socioeconomic Development of the Techiman Metropolitan area and beyond.

column14: Otumfuo Osei Tutu II Technical Institute is abbreviated as OTTECH in Adako Jachie and Kwamo locality under Ejisu Municipality. OTTECH is just by Accra-Kumasi between Ejisu and Kumasi.
column15:
Otumfuo Osei Tutu II Technical Institute is a Category "A" co-educational second-cycle technical school located in Adako Jachie-Kwamo in the Ashanti Region of Ghana. The school was established in September 1978 by the Department of Community Development under the ministry of Local Government and Rural Development. 

The institute has undergone three name transitions: it was initially called Center for Rural Industries (CRI) alongside with Kwamo Technical Institute (Kwatech), and later changed to Community Development Technical Institute (CoDTECH), from there to Adako Jachie Technical Institute (AJATECH) and now Otumfuo Osei Tutu II Technical Institute (OTTECH). 

We offer seven (7) programmes namely: Electrical Engineering Technology, Building Construction Technology, Plumbing and Gas Technology, Automotive Engineering Technology, Welding and Fabrication Technology, Garment/Fashion Design Technology and Wood Technology. 
The school write both CTVET CERTIFICATE II Examination and National Proficiency Examination.

Otumfuo Osei Tutu II Technical Institute is Mixed School with Day and Boarding System.

St. Basilide’s Vocational Technical Institute was founded by the Brothers of the Immaculate Conception (FIC) under the sponsorship of the Catholic Diocese of Wa
When the idea was mooted in 1972 no formal vocational or technical school existed in the Upper West Region
column15:
St. Basilide’s Vocational Technical Institute was founded by the Brothers of the Immaculate Conception (FIC) under the sponsorship of the Catholic Diocese of Wa. The Institute was officially opened on 5th February 1973 by Bro. Jos Van Dinther. The idea of a technical training institute of this nature came from Brothers John van Winden, Henry schurs (Borgias) and Jos Van dinther in their concern for the growth and development of people.
At the time brothers were building Nandom senior High School, they faced the difficulty of recruiting trained and/or competent craftsmen, which compelled them to recruit skilled labour from the southern part of Ghana for employment. This experience brought about the need to build and develop the potentials of the youth through skills training in the Northern sector, Wa to be précised.
When the idea was mooted in 1972 no formal vocational or technical school existed in the Upper West Region which was then Upper Region. The nearest vocational and technical education and training was in Bawku and Tamale about 381km and 386km away respectively.
The realization of a non-existent skilled labour was obvious, which necessitated the establishment of a practical oriented vocational training centre in Kaleo for the transformation of the area and its development.
The Institute has expanded in its infrastructural and institutional process of development. These changes have been necessitated by the unprecedented interest of teeming JHS leavers to develop their potential in capacity building related to competency-based occupational skills.
In 1st September 2010, the school was absorbed into the public institutions and to be supported by government GES (Ghana Education Service). This whole idea of absorption of St Basilide’s Technical Institute into the public system was a way to partner with government in training the students 
One dire need at the time was the untrained Middle School Leavers who were not having any technical education and therefore cannot meet the requirements of the job market. This resulted in their migration to the southern part of Ghana in search of jobs in the gold mines and the cocoa plantations.
The institute currently has six very attractive courses which include the following:

Welding and Fabrication Technology 
Building Construction Technology 
Wood Construction Technology 
Electrical Electronics Engineering Technology.
Fashion and Design Technology 
Solar Voltaic Engineering Technology

VISION STATEMENT
To become economically and socially sustainable TVET system, delivering to Ghanaian youth knowledge, skills and gospel values that support a productive life engagement and responsible citizenship.

MISSION STATEMENT 
To train highly proficient technical graduates with sound morals for self or formal employment using effective or sustainable approaches
OUR CORE VALUES
Commitment and enthusiasm
Solidarity and responsibility for social justice 
Accountability
Innovation and creativity
Transparency
Discipline
Credibility
Integrity
Competence
Challenges.
Despite these successes the institute still grabbling with the following challenges:
Lack of fence wall
Inadequate classrooms
Library
Small size Workshops for practical training
Lack of school clinic 
Lack of modern machines/Tools for practical training 
Rickety school bus
Given these challenges, the Institute remains resilient  with delivery of technical education and training

column15:
Takoradi Technical Institute formerly known as Takoradi Vocational 
Training and Trade Promotion Centre was born out of an agreement 
signed between the Government of Ghana and the German Government 
for technical cooperation (GTZ) to provide skilled labour for the 
industrial development for the Western Region and Ghana at large.
Initially, its primary objective was to polish the skills of artisans such as 
Mechanics, Welders etc. within the Sekondi-Takoradi Metropolis. 
In March 1982, the Ghana Education Service through the 
Technical/Vocational Education Division, converted the Centre into a 
technical school and reviewed the rather long name of Takoradi 
Vocational Training and Trade Promotion Centre to Takoradi 
Technical Institute (TTI) to conform to the names of other sister 
technical training Institutes.
In October 1982, TTI started operation as a full-fledged technical 
institute for academic work with three departments namely;
1. Mechanical Engineering with fifteen (15) students
2. Electrical Engineering with twelve (12) students
3. Automotive Engineering was still running as a mobile workshop for 
auto mechanics in the Sekondi-Takoradi Metropolis

column15:

Kumawu Technical Institute is one of the 34 National Vocational Training Institutes (NVTI) schools in Ghana. NVTI is a Ghana Government Agency that has the mandate to coordinate all aspects of vocational training, including apprenticeship, testing and certification in Ghana. The Institute is located in the Sekyere Kumawu District of Ashanti Region in Ghana. The school runs courses for both male, female youths and adults in General Electricals, Masonry (Building Construction) and Fashion and Design Technology. column2: St. John’s Technical Institute (SJTI) in Nandom was founded in 1973 by the Brothers of Immaculate Conception (FIC), a Catholic religious congregation, originally as the Nandom Practical Vocational School. It has since grown into a public Technical/ Vocational Education and Training (TVET) offering both day and boarding facilities, with a mission to empower students through practical skills for self-sufficiency and development.
column14: St. John’s Technical Institute, Nandom, founded in 1973 by the Brothers of Immaculate Conception, is a Catholic-based vocational and technical secondary school in Ghana’s Upper West Region. It offers mixed day and boarding education, while maintaining Catholic values, and practical skills to empower students for self-sufficiency.
column15:
St. John’s Technical Institute (SJTI) in Nandom is a Catholic-founded vocational and technical secondary school established in 1973. For over five decades, it has provided mixed day and boarding education, focusing on practical skills, discipline, and self-sufficiency, while currently addressing infrastructure needs through its Golden Jubilee classroom expansion project.
Historical Background
• 	Founded: 1973
• 	Founder: Brothers of Immaculate Conception (FIC), a Catholic religious congregation.
• 	Original Name: Nandom Practical Vocational School.
• 	Transformation: Later renamed St. John’s Vocational/Technical Institute to align with Ghana Education Service standards.
• 	Golden Jubilee: Celebrated in 2023, marking 50 years of service, with a major project to expand classrooms and improve facilities.
Academic & Student Life
• 	Gender: Mixed (boys and girls)
• 	Residential Status: Day and boarding facilities available
• 	Mission Statement: Empowering students with knowledge and skills for a productive and self-sufficient life.
• 	Focus: Technical and vocational training, preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship.

column44:
1. Classroom Deficit
• 	The school struggles with inadequate classroom space, limiting student intake.
• 	This challenge prompted the launch of the Golden Jubilee Classroom Expansion Project to accommodate growing enrollment.
2. Infrastructure Development Needs
• 	Beyond classrooms, facilities such as workshops, dormitories, and ICT labs require modernization.
• 	Limited resources slow down the pace of upgrading technical training environments.
3. Funding & Resource Mobilization
• 	Reliance on alumni, community support, and government allocations makes consistent funding difficult.
• 	Sustaining vocational programs and expanding infrastructure requires stronger financial backing.
column45:
1. Infrastructure Expansion
• 	The Golden Jubilee classroom project provides a chance to modernize facilities.
• 	Expanding workshops, ICT labs, and dormitories will enhance technical training and attract more students.
2. Alumni & Community Engagement
• 	Strong alumni networks can drive fundraising, mentorship, and job placement opportunities.
• 	Community partnerships can strengthen the school’s role as a hub for vocational development in the Upper West Region.
3. Curriculum Diversification & Technology Integration
• 	Introducing new technical programs (ICT, renewable energy, modern trades) can align with Ghana’s evolving job market.
• 	Digital learning tools and partnerships with industries can boost employability and innovation among students.
column46:
1. Infrastructure Development
• 	Immediate investment in classroom expansion to address deficits.
• 	Modernization of workshops, ICT labs, and dormitories to meet current technical training standards.
• 	Provision of specialized equipment for vocational programs such as carpentry, electricals, catering, and ICT.
2. Funding & Resource Allocation
• 	Increased government funding for technical and vocational education in the Upper West Region.
• 	Dedicated grants for Golden Jubilee projects and future infrastructure needs.
• 	Support for scholarships and bursaries to ensure access for students from low-income families.
3. Curriculum & Staff Development
• 	Expansion of curriculum to include modern trades (renewable energy, ICT, digital skills).
• 	Continuous teacher training and professional development to align with industry standards.
• 	Partnerships with industries for internships, apprenticeships, and job placement.
column47:
1. Curriculum Development & Exchange
• 	Partner with other technical institutes to share expertise in modern trades such as ICT, renewable energy, and digital skills.
• 	Joint curriculum reviews to align with industry standards and national TVET policies.
2. Infrastructure & Resource Sharing
• 	Collaborate with nearby vocational schools to share workshops, laboratories, and specialized equipment.
• 	Joint projects for infrastructure development, reducing costs and maximizing resources.
3. Research & Innovation
• 	Work with universities and polytechnics on applied research projects in technical fields.
• 	Encourage student innovation competitions across institutes to foster creativity and entrepreneurship.
4. Teacher Training & Professional Development
• 	Exchange programs for teachers to upgrade skills and learn new teaching methods.
• 	Collaborative workshops and seminars to strengthen technical instruction.
5. Student Exchange & Joint Programs
• 	Organize exchange programs with other institutes to broaden student exposure.
• 	Joint technical fairs, exhibitions, and competitions to showcase skills and build networks.

column14: Asankare Technical Institute is a TVET institute located at the outskirts of the Asankare and Atwidie townships. It was established in 1986 to provide Agricultural, Technical and Vocational Education to the youth in and around Asante Akyem. Currently, we run the following courses; Agriculture Mechanization Technology, Building Construction Technology, Electrical Engineering Technology, Fashion Design Technology, Hospitality and Catering Management, Plumbing and Gas Fitting Technology, Wood Construction Technology
column15:
SCHOOL PROFILE
Location:  
Asankare Technical Institute is located in Asankare, a town in the Asante Akyem South District in the Ashanti Region of Ghana.
History:  
Asankare Technical Institute formerly known as the Youth Leadership and Skills Training Institute of Asankare was establish on 20 October 1986 on an abandoned road construction camp site of Messer’s Kasadjan by the effort of Asante Regional Coordinator, the District Secretary, the late Omanhene of Asankare and the late Asantehene Otumfuor Opoku Ware II, to provide Agricultural, Technical and Vocational Education to the youth in and around Asante Akyem. A hundred (100) acres of land was released to the school. 
The school has gone through different phases in its quest to make it better and to deliver its mandate of empowering the youth through the acquisition of employable skills. However, the school has now realigned to Ghana TVET service to create a conducive learning environment that fosters academic excellence, character development, and social responsibility among our students.
Asankare Technical Institute has grown into a reputable institution known for equipping students with practical and industry-relevant skills.
Programs Offered: 
The institute offers a variety of technical and vocational programs designed to meet the demands of the job market. Some of the key programs include:
Electrical Engineering Technology
Agriculture Mechanization Technology
Mechanical Engineering (Automobile)
Fashion Design and Textile Technology
Carpentry and Joinery
Plumbing Technology
Welding and Fabrication
Facilities:  
The school for some time now has been battling with inadequate modern facilities that support hands-on training, but can also boast of facilities such as:
School farms
ICT labs
Classrooms
Fashion, Electrical and Catering workshops
Mission:  
The mission of Asankare Technical Institute is to provide high-quality ATVET skills and services that are demand-driven and industry-led to persons of all ages and careers, preparing them to be productive citizens contributing to social and economic development through competency-based training, apprenticeship, testing and career-development
Achievements:  
The school has gained recognition for its commitment to technical excellence. Many graduates of the institute have gone on to pursue careers in industry, while others have established successful entrepreneurial ventures.
Extracurricular Activities:  
In addition to academics, the school encourages student participation in extracurricular activities such as sports, debates, and clubs to foster holistic development.
Admission Requirements:  
Admission to the school is open to students who have completed Junior High School (JHS) and have passed the Basic Education Certificate Examination (BECE). Prospective students can apply based on their interest in Agriculture, Technical and Vocational Training.
Affiliations:  
Asankare Technical Institute is affiliated with Ghana's Commission for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (CTVET) and operates under the supervision of the Ghana TVET Service.
Contact Information:
For further inquiries, the school can be contacted via asatechinst@gmail.com, 0244983324 or 0244989792

column14: Methodist Technical Institute, Kwadaso was established in the year 1992. The Institute is located in the Ashanti Region in  Kwadaso Municipality. The Institute offers 12 courses namely; Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Hardware, Mechanical Engineering, Welding & Fabrication, Automobile, Building Construction, Wood Construction, Fashion Design, Catering and Hospitality and Plumbing and Gas Technology under Ghana Free TVET program. It is located 100 meters from the Church of Pentecost, Kwadaso.
column15:
The Methodist Technical Institute (MTI), Kwadaso was established in 1992.  The history of the institute cannot be told without mentioning Mr. Sampson Fobi [to whom House two (2) was named after], the brain behind the establishment of the institution. He was worshipping with the Bethel Methodist Church, Kwadaso before he travelled to Germany to seek greener pastures in 1979. While there, he associated himself with Mr. and Mrs. Shudder Uwe and the Lutheran Church where he appealed to the Lutheran Church about improving youth education in Ghana.
The educational request gave birth to Kwadaso Bethel Methodist School, which is now known as Methodist Technical Institute, Kwadaso, which was aimed at helping the numerous needy youth and school drop-outs in the Church and its environs. The items he requested for were carpentry tools and equipment. On granting the request, the items were brought to Bethel Methodist Church, Kwadaso in 1990.
It is worth noting that Mr. and Mrs. Shudder Uwe [to whom House three (3) was named, were leading members of the Lutheran Church, made generous and substantial contributions towards the purchase of the educational equipment. The construction of the workshop structure for the tools began in 1991 by the Bethel Methodist Church, Kwadaso under the leadership of Very Rev. Benjamin K. Asare (and subsequently, the establishment of the institute in 1992). This project was completed by Very Rev. Asua Sekyere in 1997, and subsequently, the establishment of the institute in 1998. 
In 2009/2010, the Bishop of Kumasi Diocese, Rt. Rev. Osei Safo Kantanka and Very Rev. Agyenim Boateng, the Superintendent Minister of Kwadaso Circuit, whilst acting on Synod’s decision, handed over the institute to the Government of Ghana for operational management by the Ghana Education Service (GES). This was when the name changed from Bethel Methodist School to Methodist Technical Institute, Kwadaso-Kumasi.
The Kumasi Diocese of the Methodist Church Ghana and the German counterparts led by Mr. and Mrs. Schudder have helped in ways such as the construction of the Boys’ dormitory, the construction of classrooms (MDF Block), and others.

column15:
Bolgatanga Central Technical Institute is a public technical institution located in Bolgatanga, Upper East Region, Ghana. Established in 1972 by the Department of Community Development, and absorbed by Ghana TVET Service in February 2021. BCTI located in the Upper East Region of Ghana, has a rich history of providing technical education to the youth. Established to predominamtly to equip students with practical skills, the institution has grown over the years to become a reputable center for technical and vocational training 

BCTI  is a training institution dedicated in equipping the youth with employable skills in various vocational and technical areas. including:
1.Welding and Fabrication Technology
2.Electrical Engineering Technology
3.Building Construction Technology
4.Fashion Designing Technology
5.Cosmetology
6.Hospitality & Catering Management
7.Business Secretarial

Other Pilot Trades Include
8.Solar Photovoltaic Technology
9. Motor Vehicle Engineering

The institute offers in-plant training opportunities for all trades and is also a center for piloting new trades. BCTI has partnered with other reputable businesses, and  companies in the Upper East Region to provide trainees with industrial experience and employment opportunities.

Mission Statement
To provide quality technical education, fostering technical competencies, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills in students, enabling them to succeed in their chosen careers.

Vision Statement
Empowering students through skills development to become self-reliant leaders, transforming the region and thriving in a dynamic global landscape.

column14:
Ada Technical Institute is a public TVET school in Greater Accra Region of Ghana.
Established in 1982, it offers practical training in Engineering, Construction, Fashion, and Business. The institute equips students with hands-on skills and knowledge for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education in technical and vocational fields.
column15:
Ada Technical Institute is a public TVET school which was established in the year 1982 and it is located at Atortorkope near Big Ada in the Greater Accra Region. 
The institute provides practical skills-based education aimed at preparing students for employment and national development. Its mission focuses on delivering quality technical training to support Ghana's socio-economic growth.
The institute offers a range of technical and business-related programmes, such as Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Mechanical and Automobile Engineering, Building Construction, Wood Technology, Fashion Design, Business Studies etc.
With over 2000 students and a mix of teaching and non-teaching staff, the institute is equipped with facilities such as workshops, classrooms, a computer lab, and a library to support hands-on learning.

column15:
INSTITUTIONAL PROFILE
Name: Hemang Buoho Technical Institute
Type: Senior High School
Location: Hemang Buoho, Ashanti - Ghana
Vision
To provide the best systems of employable technical and vocational education and training skills
Mission
To provide demand driven employable skills and enhance the income generating capacities of basic and secondary school leavers and such other persons through competency based apprentiship, mastery craftmanship, testing and career development.
History
The institution was built in 2000. It was started in an old town house in Hemang - Buoho, Ashanti Region of Ghana. It started with two(2) courses- General Electricals and Building and construction with the core subjects being English, Mathematics, Science, entreprenuership and  Information and communication technology. Later, it was relocated to a new place with three classroom blocks and an administration office which was built by the district assembly in 2012. With the help of community members and parents the building and construction department was able to add additional classroom blocks which served as workshops. In 2018, ghana and germany had an agreement to support technical and vocational training in the country and as a result the school had some additional facilities which include; an administration block, two classroom blocks,ICT lab, three workshops building with modern tools and equipments for trainning, hundred bed capacity boarding house for boys and 50 bed caapcity boarding house for girls. Before 2018, it was fee paying institution and so it had few students about 90 but with the introduction of free senior high school, we now currently have over 650 students as regular and over 50 running short courses in various programs. The school now has 37 teaching and non-teching staff 
Programs: Electrical and Electronic Technology, Building and Construction Technology, Plumbing and Gas Technology, Catering and Hospitality Management, Fashion and Information Technology
Achievements:
From a Category D to Category C school
Regional best Electrical and Electronic Technology School
2 nd runner up on Environgreen competition
Challenges 
The school still has numerous challenges. These include;
Security issues - no fence wall, limited number of security officers
Inadequate teaching and non - teaching staff
Electrical power cuts
No internet services for students
The current curriculum is too theoritical

column14: Bechem Technical Institute is a technical and vocational institute located in Bechem in the Ahafo Region of Ghana. its main objective is to promote skills development by equipping learners with employable and competence training required by the job market and also promoting entrepreneurship .
column15:
Bechem Technical Institute is a public institute located in the Ahafo Region of Ghana.

Its vision is to be a world-class TVET institution dedicated to the development of well rounded, skilled, confident and responsible persons who strive to reach their full potential with equitable opportunity.
Its mission is to encourage academic career technical, and vocational excellence into better equip students to enter and remain competitive in global workforce.

The institution started as the Women's Training Institute in 1966 as a single sex training centre. It transformed to  Bechem Community Development Vocational and Technical Institute, a mixed sex institution. After the merging of all TVET institutions under the Ghana TVET Service, the institution was renamed to Bechem Technical Institute in 2022. 

The institute is a category B TVET school for both boys and girls. It is free to attend under GTVET. The institute runs 10 technical programs namely Catering& Hospitality, cosmetology, Fashion and Garment design, Engineering (electrical, computer hardware, welding and fabrication, and automotive), Construction (building, wood, plumbing and gas fitting).

The institute is headed by a principal and assisted by two vice principals. It has a student population size larger than 1,000 and a staff strength of 33.

column14: Ho Technical Institute is a pre-tertiary TVET institution in Ho in the Volta Region. Courses offered in the institute are Electrical Engineering Technology, Hospitality and Catering management, Building Construction Technology, Fashion Designing Technology and Wood Construction Technology. It is both a day and boarding school and offers admission to both males and females. The school is populated with over 850 students and over 50 working staff.
column15:
Ho Technical Institute is a pre-tertiary TVET institution located in Ho in the Volta Region of Ghana. Ho Technical Institute was once called Social Welfare Vocational and Training Centre. When TVET was fully established and brought under one umbrella under the Ministry of Education. The school was captured and the name of the school was changed to Ho Technical Institute in the year 2021. Prior to the name change, the programmes that were offered in the school were purely NVTI courses, where when students graduate from the school, they are awarded with Certificate 1. Currently, the NVTI programmes are not run in the school except TVET programmes. Students are awarded with National Certificate 2 after they write their CTVET examination. This certificate grants them the opportunity to advance into the various technical universities in the country. Students are well taught in the school with the required knowledge, skills, values and attitudes that will equip them for their future endeavors in entrepreneurship and in the job market. 
Students in Ho Technical Institute go for for workplace experience learning (WEL) when they are on vacation for them to have a glimpse of how the industry works and also to help them learn with advanced systems in the industry. The admission rate of the school has increased over time as well as the number of staff. The current population of students is over 850 and the current population of staff is over 50. Due to inadequate infrastructure, the school runs a double track system in order to cater for the number of students. The school has both day and boarding facilities. Aside, academic work, students are excellent in co-curricular activities such as volleyball, football and the arts.

column14: Social Welfare Technical Institute is a TVET institute that provides knowledge and hands-on training skills, such as electrical, fashion, building technology, hospitality catering, and the core subjects. The institute is located in Sunyani, Bono Region.
column15:
Established in 1974, the Social Welfare Technical Institute is a leading Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Sunyani, Bono Region. For over five decades, the institute has been dedicated to empowering students by providing a unique blend of theoretical knowledge and intensive hands-on practical skills.

The institute offers a comprehensive range of technical programmes designed to meet industrial demands and foster self-reliance. These include:

* Electrical Engineering Technology
* Garment/Fashion Design Technology
* Building Construction Technology
* Catering and Hospitality Management
* Textiles Design Technology
* Automotive Engineering Technology
* Cosmetology
* Woodwork Technology
* Fine Arts Technology
* Leather Work Upholstery
* Plumbing and Gas Technology
* Welding and Fabrication

In addition to these specialized trades, the institute integrates essential Core Subjects into its curriculum to ensure every graduate possesses a well-rounded educational foundation. 

Our training philosophy centers on competency-based learning, bridging the gap between the classroom and the world of work. As a vital educational hub in the Bono Region, the Social Welfare Technical Institute remains committed to transforming students into skilled professionals, craftsmen, and entrepreneurs who contribute significantly to national development. Through decades of experience, we continue to uphold our reputation as a premier destination for practical, career-focused education in Ghana.

column14: ABIRIW PRESBYTERIAN TECHNICAL INSTITUTE is a Faith-based and an agency of Ghana TVET Service under the Ministry of  Education with the support from the Presbyterian church of Ghana. The School has been providing employable skills training to the  youth and adults in Abiriw and its surrounding communities and Ghana as a whole since 2003. Across Abiriw and surrounding communities, ABIRIW PRESBY  has graduated hundreds of trainees who have successfully entered the workforce in their respective communities, easing the burden of unemployment as well as the rural-urban drift that has challenged the country. The school is located at Abiriw-Akuapem in the Okere District, Eastern Region. The School currently run four Programs i.e. Electrical Engineering Technology, Fashion Design Technology, Hair Technology and Catering and Hospitality Management.
column15:
Initially dubbed “Presby Women Teens Centre” the school was started by the Women Fellowship of the Calvary Presbyterian Church, Abiriw in 1990 as a skills acquisition (apprenticeship) Centre for teenage mothers in palm kernel-oil production and soap making, aimed at curtailing teenage pregnancy  that had bedevilled the community, causing lots of girls dropping out of school to nurse their babies.  Members of the Presby Women Fellowship led by Mrs. Susana Siaw and Mrs Yeboah Dankwa lobbied the Planned Parenthood Association of Ghana (PPAG) who had been operating in Akuapem since 1983, to provide support. This partnership brought relief as the PPAG provided clinical services (i.e. family planning services, counselling (sex education), antenatal care, mother and child welfare and sponsorship). This led to an increased enrolment of teenage mothers and single mothers at the Centre. To accommodate this growth, in 1993, the PPAG renovated an old building (now serving as male hostel) and a shed (now used as the General office cum District Minister’s office) into office and training Centre, to house a branch office  and changed the name of the Centre to Teenage Rehabilitation Centre, focused on Family Life Education, Catering, Dressmaking, as well as Batik and “tie-and-dye” for teen school dropouts and young unmarried mothers. The pioneer class had twenty-five (25) young mothers enrolled to undertake a three (3) year programme in vocational skills such as sewing, bread baking and handicraft. 

Although only five (5) young mothers were able to go through the three year training successfully, JICA and Ghana International Bank (GHIB) took interest in the project and provided grants to complete the first phase of the classroom block building and the current female hostel of the school respectively. This was in the year 1999 and 2003 respectively. In 2010, Mr. Kofi Otutu Adu Labi through one Helen Shipton, a Scottish solicitor, again secured another funds to complete the first floor. 


As the enrolment soared, PPAG handed over the vocational aspect to the Government by going into collaboration with the Integrated Community Centres for Employable Skills (ICCES), an agency of Ministry of Employment and Labour Relations in the year 2003.

ICCES and PPAG jointly administered the Centre from 2003 to 2007 when PPAG winded up their Reproductive Health project in the District and handed over the school to the Akuapem Presbytery of the Presbyterian Church of Ghana. 
Thus, the school’s name evolved from Presbyterian Women Centre, to PPAG Teens Centre, Presby Resource Centre, Presby Vocational Technical Institute, Presbyterian ICCES Vocational & Technical Institute and presently, Abiriw Presbyterian Technical Institute.

The Pre-tertiary Education Act 1049 of 2020 has merged about 255 public TVET Institutions under different Ministries to Ministry of Education. 
The Merged TVET institutions have TVET Service to manage, oversee and ensure the implementation of approved national policies and program relating to pre-tertiary technical and vocational education and training. 
The school, since 2022, has now changed policy management from Integrated Community Centres for Employable skills (ICCES) under the Ministry of Employment and Labour Relation to TVET service under the Ministry of Education. The school currently is part of the Free SHS/TVET policy and receives full support from the Ghana TVET Service and Ministry of Education.

column14: St. Daniel Comboni Technical Institute, established in 1981 by the Comboni Missionaries, is a leading TVET institution in Ghana. It offers diverse technical programs, training over 800 students. With a strong reputation for skills development, the institute faces growing infrastructure needs due to increased enrollment and demand for technical education.
column15:
St. Daniel Comboni Technical Institute is a reputable Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution established on 23rd September 1981 by the Comboni Missionaries, a Catholic congregation founded by St. Daniel Comboni. Located on a vast land area of approximately 20.62 acres, the institute has grown steadily over the years to become a center of excellence for technical skills development in the Volta Region and Ghana at large.

The institute began its journey as a trade school, officially opening its doors on 22nd October 1981 with an initial intake of fifty (50) students drawn mainly from the Liati community and surrounding areas such as Have, Nyagbo, Wudome, Gbledi, and Logba. Notable among the pioneering students were Ing. Francis Dotse and Ing. Eric Adoboe. At its inception, the school was managed by Rev. Fr. Eugenio Petrogalli, with Mr. Festus Tornyesinu serving as the first Principal. The academic work was supported by a dedicated team of eight (8) skilled teachers who delivered training in five core trade areas: Bakery, Fashion Designing, Carving, Carpentry and Joinery, and Blacksmithing.

Over the decades, the institute has undergone significant transformation, evolving from a basic trade school into a fully-fledged technical institute. Today, it offers nine (9) diverse and industry-relevant programs, including Electrical Engineering Technology, Automotive Engineering Technology, Building Construction Technology, Wood Construction Technology, Catering and Hospitality Technology, Fashion Design Technology, Architectural Draftsmanship, Agricultural Mechanization Technology, and Welding and Fabrication Technology. These programs are designed to equip students with practical skills, innovation, and entrepreneurship competencies to meet the demands of the modern job market.

The introduction of the Free TVET policy and increased national interest in technical education have significantly boosted student enrollment, growing from 120 students in 2016 to over 800 currently. While this growth reflects the institute’s strong reputation and impact, it has also placed considerable pressure on existing infrastructure and resources. Despite its long history of over four decades, the institute has seen limited infrastructural development, creating challenges in accommodating the growing student population.

In particular, the need for expanded facilities such as a modern dining and assembly hall has become urgent. Addressing these infrastructure deficits is critical for enhancing teaching and learning, improving student welfare, and sustaining the institute’s mission of delivering quality technical education. St. Daniel Comboni Technical Institute remains committed to shaping skilled professionals and contributing meaningfully to Ghana’s socio-economic development.

column46:
1. Infrastructure Development
Urgent support is needed for the construction and expansion of key facilities, especially a modern dining/assembly hall, additional classrooms, workshops, and student accommodation. This will help address congestion and improve the overall teaching and learning environment.

2. Provision of Modern Training Equipment
The institute requires up-to-date tools, machines, and workshop equipment aligned with current industry standards across all program areas. This will enhance practical training and ensure students graduate with relevant, employable skills.

3. Industry Linkages and Policy Support
Government facilitation in building partnerships with industries for internships, apprenticeships, and job placements is crucial. Strengthening policy frameworks that support TVET institutions will also enhance the institute’s growth and relevance.

Establish staff exchange programs to allow instructors to share knowledge, teaching methods, and technical expertise. Joint training workshops and seminars can strengthen capacity in emerging technologies and modern pedagogical approaches.

2. Digital Learning and Resource Sharing
Collaborate on e-learning platforms, digital content development, and online training resources. Sharing teaching materials, recorded lessons, and ICT tools can enhance access to quality education across institutions.

column14: Founded in 1997, Fr. Dogli Memorial Technical Institute is a premier technical school in the Oti Region. Crowned 2025 National Best Senior High School in Practical Agriculture, we provide hands-on mastery in engineering and technology. Guided by integrity, commitment and selflessness, we empower students for professional excellence and global impact.
column15:
Fr. Dogli Memorial Technical Institute (DOTECH) is a premier, government-assisted Catholic technical institution located in New Ayoma, within the Jasikan Municipality of the Oti Region.
Established on March 1, 1997, the institute has evolved from a diocesan trade school into a leading public center for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) under the Ghana Education Service.

Vision and Mission
Our vision is to remain an economically and socially sustainable institution that provides high-quality, relevant skills and intellectual training grounded in Gospel values.
We are committed to empowering the youth through hard work, selflessness, and collaboration, ensuring they acquire employable skills and sound moral foundations for sustainable livelihoods.

Academic Excellence and Distinctions
DOTECH is widely recognized for its "hands-on" pedagogical approach, summarized by our motto: Per Manibus Ad Gloriam Dei (Through our hands to the glory of God) The institute is a powerhouse in agricultural education, recently crowned the 2025 National Best Senior High School in Practical Agriculture—a title it has successfully defended multiple times since 1999. Furthermore, our students consistently excel nationally, notably producing the Overall Best Student in Catering and Hospitality Management at the 2021 Ghana TVET Excellence Awards.

Curriculum and Facilities
The institute offers a robust array of Certificate II – Intermediate programs.
Beyond core academic subjects like English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and Social Studies, students specialize in diverse elective technologies, including:
•Engineering & Construction: Electrical Engineering, Building and Construction, Plumbing, and Welding.
•Creative & Service Arts: Fashion Designing, Cosmetology, and Hospitality Management.
•Technology & Business: Computer Hardware, Digital Design, and Business (Accounting & Secretarial Options)
Our 93-hectare campus supports these programs with specialized machine shops, Demonstration Labs, and a computer laboratory. As a mixed-gender boarding and day facility, we provide a holistic environment featuring a library, dormitories, and sports facilities for football and volleyball.

Our Community, Governance & Student Life
Our progress is guided by a vibrant Board of Governors and a dedicated staff of 60, ensuring a stable foundation for institutional growth. Supported by a proactive Parent-Teacher Association (PTA) and the New Ayoma Traditional Authority, DOTECH thrives on a vibrant community spirit and a collaborative environment where every student reaches their full potential. With a current population of 1,178 students, our Guidance and Counseling Unit further ensures the spiritual and emotional growth of every individual, fostering a holistic atmosphere dedicated to the "total person."

column15:
Tutu Methodist Technical Institute is a reputable vocational and technical education institution located in Akuapem North. The institute is committed to providing quality, skills-based education that empowers students to become competent professionals and self-reliant individuals. With a strong emphasis on practical training, the school plays a vital role in developing a skilled workforce to support local and national development.
The institute offers a range of career-focused programs designed to meet the demands of various industries. These include Electrical Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Building and Construction, Catering and Hospitality, and Fashion Design Technology. Each program combines theoretical knowledge with hands-on experience, ensuring that students acquire the technical expertise and confidence needed to excel in their chosen fields
In addition to technical skills, the institute places a strong emphasis on discipline, integrity, and personal development. Students are encouraged to cultivate professionalism, teamwork, and entrepreneurial thinking. This holistic approach ensures that graduates are not only skilled workers but also responsible citizens capable of contributing positively to society.

First batch of 65 student and still counting
column15:
Juaben Technical Institute is a fast-growing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution established in 2024 to provide practical, skills-based education to young people. The institute was founded with a clear vision of equipping students with relevant technical competencies, entrepreneurial skills, and professional discipline needed to thrive in today’s competitive job market.

At its inception, the institute began with two core programmes: Electrical Engineering Technology and Plumbing and Gas Fitting Technology. These programmes were carefully selected to address critical skill gaps in the technical sector and to provide students with hands-on training that aligns with industry demands. Building on its early success and growing reputation, the institute has recently expanded its academic offerings to include Fashion Design Technology and Building Construction Technology. This expansion reflects the institute’s commitment to diversifying opportunities and meeting the evolving interests and career aspirations of its students.

Currently, Juaben Technical Institute operates with two academic levels—first-year and second-year students—with a total student population of approximately 200. The institute maintains a conducive learning environment supported by nine well-utilized classrooms that facilitate both theoretical instruction and practical engagement.

The institution is staffed by a dedicated team of 17 personnel, comprising 10 teaching staff and 7 non-teaching staff. The teaching staff are committed professionals who bring both academic knowledge and industry experience into the classroom, ensuring that students receive quality, competency-based education. The non-teaching staff provide essential administrative and operational support, contributing to the smooth running of the institute.

Juaben Technical Institute is a day school, making education accessible to students within Juaben and surrounding communities. The campus is designed to support student welfare and convenience, including a spacious area allocated for food vendors who provide a variety of meals for both students and staff. Additionally, the institute is equipped with a six-seater water closet (WC) toilet facility, ensuring proper sanitation and hygiene on campus.

A unique feature of the institute is its 2.5-acre farmland, which serves as a resource for practical learning, potential agricultural projects, and future expansion. This space provides opportunities for students to engage in supplementary skills development and promotes sustainability initiatives.

In summary, Juaben Technical Institute stands as a promising center for technical education, committed to excellence, innovation, and the holistic development of its students.

column14: Nyohini Community Technical Institute is a technical and vocational education institution located in Tamale, Ghana. The school offers various TVET training programmes geared toward practical skills development and self reliability for the youth.
column15:
Nyohini Community Technical Institute is the new name of the Institute formerly known as Community Development Vocational and Technical Training Institute. The Institute was established in 1966 with the name Women’s Training Institute as a government institution belonging to the Department of Community Development under the Ministry of Local Government, Decentralization and Rural Development (MLGDRD).

The sole aim and objective of the Institute was to provide young ladies with vocational skills to make them self reliant and supportive to their families. However, over time, the mandate of the Institute in face of global integration and changing educational reforms has been expanded to include technical training modules which have resulted in attracting youth particular young boys into the Institute.

Currently, the Institute has been absorbed by the Ghana TVET Service as its mandate to streamline all TVET institutions under one body to ensure effectiveness and efficiency in the sector.

Courses offered by the Institute are structured to prevent or decrease the high incidence of youth migration to urban cities in search of white-coloured jobs, through the provision of vocational and technical skills training.

column14: Adrobaa Technical Institute was established as an integrated community centre for Employable Skills (I.C.C.E.S.) on the 3rd day of November 2015 an agency under the Ministry of Employment and Labour Relations (MELR) an initiative of Nana Odiasempa Kwaku Buadu II the Adrobaamanhene and his elders in collaboration with Mr. George Attipoe the Regional coordinator and was given a departmental status in 2020. It then became Department of Integrated Community Centres for Employable Skills (D.I. C.C. E.S.). It is now being realigned to Ghana TVET service under the Ministry of Education.
column15: Adrobaa Technical Institute is a vocational and technical training institution located in Adrobaa, within the Tano North Municipality of the Ahafo Region, Ghana. The school is a public TVET institution formed in the year 2015 by Integrated Community Centre for Employable Skills (ICCES) spearheaded by the Chiefs and stakeholders of the Adrobaa Community. The institute runs three (3) programmes in areas such as Electrical Engineering Technology, Building Construction Technology and Fashion Designing Technology with ICT inclusion thereby providing employable skills to the youth in the community, Ahafo region, Ghana and beyond. Adrobaa Technical institute started as a mixed day school in 2015 but was later approved to run both day and a boarding in the 2025, 2026 academic year. The institute currently operates under the Ghana TVET Service an agency of the Ministry of Education established by the TVET Service Act, 2020 (Act 1049) to manage pre-tertiary technical and vocational education. The institute uses the slogan "Making You Employable... Our Future Is Secure" to highlight its focus on job readiness and industry-driven training. The institute is served by the Techire-Adrobaa road to better connect the town and its educational facilities to the rest of the entire municipality. The institute has become part of a broader surge in technical education in the Ahafo Region.

column14:
Tema Mission Technical Institute is a Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution regulated by the Ghana TVET Service, dedicated to providing both formal and informal training, as well as retraining, in employable skills for the youth.
A recognized certificate, designated as CERT II (Intermediate) and issued by the Commission for Technical and Vocational Education and Training is awarded to students upon the successful completion of a three-year course of study at the institute. This qualification enables TVET graduates to further their education at any recognized technical university nationwide.
column15:
The Institute was established in 1981 to offer skill training to the youth of Tema by the Industrial Mission of the Tema Local Council of Churches under the Christian Council of Ghana. It was given accreditation by the NVTI and subsequently by the Ghana Education Service to run various courses. In May, 2010, an MOU was signed between the NVTI and The Tema Local Council of Churches for the absorption of the Institute by the NVTI. Trainees were expected to have both Centre and Industrial exposure before completion of the four (4) – year training programme which led to the award of NVTI foundation Certificate, Certificate I & II, after acquiring one year of industrial practical experience.

After the realignment of all TVET Institutions by the Pre-Tertiary Education Act (2020), Act 1049 which brought All Public TVET Institutions under the Ghana TVET Service. The Institute is to provide both formal and informal training and retraining in employable skills to the youth and any other desirable skill to the youth of Tema and beyond., Tema Mission Technical Institute has been Graded as one of the Grade A Institutions Under the Service, It is also in its Fifth (5) year of running the three (3) year Free TVET program. 

A recognized certificate, designated as CERT II (Intermediate) and issued by the Commission for Technical and Vocational Education and Training is awarded to students upon the successful completion of a three-year course of study at the institute. This qualification enables TVET graduates to further their education at any recognized technical university nationwide.
