column14: Suntreso Technical Institute (STI) is a proud Public TVET Institute under the Ghana TVET Service and duly accredited by the Commission for TVET and the Energy Commission of Ghana. We lead with 50years experience in practical skills training; and so far trained over 80,000 youth.
column15:
1.0 Introduction
Established in 1976, Suntreso Technical Institute (STI) is a Government Technical/Vocational Institute of the Ministry of Education and directly operating under the Ghana Technical and Vocational Education and Training Service (Ghana TVET Service). STI is strategically located in the Kumasi Metropolis with a current student’s population of 638 and a staff strength of 72. The school’s total land size is 4.6acres.

2.0 Mission
To provide the youth with quality and innovative Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) skills that meets the industry standards.

3.0 Vision
To emerge as the most outstanding Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Institute within the Ghana TVET Service fraternity that adopts best practices and Workplace Experience Learning (WEL) approaches in its skills training delivery. 

4.0 Core Values
•	Quality
•	Diversity
•	Innovation
•	Integrity and Accountability

5.0 Target Beneficiaries
•	Mainstream: Junior High School (JHS) graduates.
•	Part -Time/Open School Programme: Workers, Senior High School (SHS) graduates and other individuals.

6.0 Skills Training, Support Services and Resource Mobilization Activities
Mainstream skills training which is a 3year Programme is designed for Junior High School (JHS) graduates who get directly enrolled into the school through the Computerized School Selection and Placement System (CSSPS) under the “Free TVET for All” Programme, whiles the part time training is basically organized between 3-6months mainly for workers, Senior High School (SHS) graduates and other individuals who wish to acquire skills training. Mainstream students sit for the Commission for TVET(CTVET) Certificate II Examinations. Part time students sit for the Proficiency Examinations.

6.1 Career Guidance and Counseling
The school has a well-resourced counseling unit with qualified personnel who provide both career guidance & counseling interventions for students. The purpose of the counseling programmes is to provide students with supportive learning environment to succeed academically, socially and emotionally; and also to equip students with the requisite counseling tools that will aide TVET graduates’ job acquisition and their ability to surmount future life challenges after completion of training. 

6.2 Workplace Experience Learning (WEL)
In order to expose students to the practical industry experience as prescribed by the TVET Service, students undergo 10 -11 weeks Workplace Experience Learning (WEL) in various WEL collaborating industries every year as part of the academic calendar. Currently, the school is partnering with more than 100 industries for the WEL Programme. Since its implementation in year 2023, about 480 students have so far benefitted from the WEL.

6.3 Programmes/Courses
•	Hospitality & Catering Management
•	Electrical Engineering Technology
•	Plumbing & Gas Fitting Technology
•	Building Construction Technology
•	Motor Vehicle Engineering
•	Computer Hardware Technology
•	Fashion Designing Technology.  

6.4 Resource Mobilization/Income Generation Activities
The available income generation ventures/services being undertaken by the Institute are:
•	Catering Services
•	Sewing of school uniforms
•	Block Molding/Production 
•	Plumbing and Electrical Works
•	3-6months part-time fee-paying training in all the seven (7) programmes
•	3 months part-time fee-paying training in Energy Commission Professional Electrical Wiring Certification (i.e Domestic, Commercial and Industrial Levels) for Electricians.
                        
7.0 Accreditation
The Institute is duly accredited by the following awarding bodies:
•	Commission for TVET
•	Energy Commission of Ghana

column14: Kumbungu Technical Institute is a leading vocational institution in Northern Ghana, distinguished for excellence in technical and skills-based education. It equips students with industry-relevant competencies, fosters innovation and entrepreneurship, and stands out for its commitment to producing highly skilled graduates for national development.
column15:
Kumbungu Technical Institute is an emerging centre of excellence in technical and vocational education in Northern Ghana. Established in 2022, the institute was founded to provide practical, industry-relevant skills training aimed at empowering young people and supporting local and national development. Since its inception, the school has demonstrated steady growth and currently enrolls a student population of 158, including more than 20 proficiency students who are being trained to enhance their competencies in various trades.

The institute currently offers programmes in Fashion Design Technology, Wood Technology, Beauty Therapy, and Hairdressing. These programmes are carefully designed to combine theoretical knowledge with hands-on practical training, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared for employment or self-employment. In line with its vision of expansion and diversification, the institute has advanced plans to introduce additional programmes such as Catering Technology, Building Technology, and Electrical Engineering to meet the growing demand for technical skills in Ghana.

Kumbungu Technical Institute is presently located at the Opportunity Industrialization Center (OIC) at the entrance of Kumbungu Township, providing a convenient and accessible learning environment for students within the municipality and beyond. The institute operates as a day school and supports student welfare by providing one hot meal per day, helping to enhance student concentration, attendance, and overall academic performance.

The school is committed to delivering high-quality technical education through a team of dedicated and qualified instructors who emphasize competency-based training. It also promotes entrepreneurship, innovation, and discipline among students, equipping them with not only technical skills but also the mindset needed to succeed in today’s competitive job market.

Through strategic partnerships, community engagement, and a focus on skills development, Kumbungu Technical Institute continues to position itself as a key player in technical and vocational education, striving to stand tall among its peers while contributing meaningfully to socio-economic development in the region.

column14: Caring Sisters’ Technical Institute is a pre-tertiary TVET institution located in Tegbi, Anloga District of the Volta Region, under the Ghana TVET Service. Established in 1990, the Institute offers Competency-Based Training in Fashion and Catering, alongside short courses, to equip learners with practical, entrepreneurial skills for employment and self-reliance.
column15:
FULL INSTITUTE PROFILE
PROGRAMMES / COURSES OFFERED
Caring Sisters’ Technical Institute offers competency-based programmes designed to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.
MAIN PROGRAMMES
•	Fashion Design and Technology
•	Catering and Hospitality Management
SHORT COURSES
The Institute also offers flexible short courses which run throughout the year. These programmes are designed to provide hands-on skills within a short period.
SHORT COURSES AVAILABLE
•	General Sewing
•	General Cookery
•	Bead Works
•	Millinery and Accessories
•	Needle Craft
•	Floral and Balloon Decoration
The duration of these short courses ranges from 3 months to 12 months, depending on the programme.

FACILITIES
1.	Workshops
The Institute has well-equipped, modern workshops for Fashion Design Technology and Catering and Hospitality Management. These workshops provide students with hands-on practical experience using industry-standard tools and equipment.

2.	ICT Laboratory
Caring Sisters’ Technical Institute has a well-equipped ICT laboratory to support teaching and learning. The laboratory is fitted with modern laptop computers, interactive screen, projector, UPS to ensure effective practical training in digital skills. 
3.	Dormitory
The Institute has a modern boarding facility that offers safe and comfortable accommodation for students.

The social welfare vocational training and rehabilitation institute was originally a rehabilitation centre established in 1972 to cater for the vocational needs of persons with disabilities to ensure that they are self-reliant, self-sufficient and have a decent livelihood.
This has been in existence for more than four decades and the Centre has turned out many persons with disabilities who are now living satisfactory and independent lives. 
In 2010, the vocational training institute and rehabilitation centre was started to provide for holistic vocational training and entrepreneurship skill to both PWD and abled bodied as part of the new social integration policy of government. 
In 2021, the name of the Centre change to Somanya Technical Institute (STI) as a result of Government educational reform agenda to re-align all technical and vocational institutions under Ghana TVET Service.
column15:
SOMANYA TECHNICAL INSTITUTE (STI)
ESTABLISHED: 1972

LOCATION: Formal Social Welfare, Near Somanya High Court

BACKGROUND OF INSTITUTE
The social welfare vocational training and rehabilitation institute was originally a rehabilitation centre established in 1972 to cater for the vocational needs of persons with disabilities to ensure that they are self-reliant, self-sufficient and have a decent livelihood.
This has been in existence for the past forty-two (42) years and the Centre has turned out many persons with disabilities who are now living satisfactory and independent lives. 
In 2010, the voc ational training institute and rehabilitation centre was started to provide for holistic vocational training and entrepreneurship skill to both PWD and abled bodied as part of the new social integration policy of government. 
In 2022, the name of the Centre change to Somanya Technical Institute (STI) as a result of Government education reform agenda to re-align all technical and vocational institutions under Ghana TVET Service.  

VISION OF THE INSTITUTE
To prepare student to attain multi-level competency and excellence to meet global standard and practice with integrity while working to promote well-being and community building. 


MISSION OF THE INSTITUTE
The Institution is committed to building a more equitable society based on values of human dignity, inclusiveness, diversity, equality, and on economic, environmental and social justice. 
To generate new knowledge by engaging in cutting-edge research and to promote growth by offering         technical education.


CORE VALUES
Academic integrity and accountability 
Respect and tolerance
Relevance as well as of global concern 
Intellectual excellence and creativity

STAFF STRENGTH
The Institute has staff population of forty-three made up of fifteen non-teaching staff and twenty-eight teaching staff members.

INFRASTRUCTURE
The institute started with the following infrastructure
Assembly Hall
3 unit classroom with ancillary toilet facilities and administration
2 unit workshop for Fashion and Beauty Therapy
Lab and offices block
2 unit block for Building Construction
2 dormitory for both boys and girls
Five (5) bungalow block for staff members 
School gate
   
TRAINING PHILOSOPHY
Somanya Technical Institute emphasizes on practical training that develops the head (Cognitive knowledge) - the heart (morality/affective) and the hands (psychomotor skills). Practical activities form the core of skills development



EDUCATIONAL TRAINING
Technical and Vocational training is both Practical and theoretical oriented. Students are always taken through vigorous theoretical lessons and some practical work. The practical training form about 70% of the training whiles theoretical work only form 30%. It is the practical activities that will bring out the skill in the trainee. The practical is the best training that touches the head -knowledge or cognitive, the heart – affective or morality and the hands - psychomotor skills 


DISCIPLINE
The Institute has 7-member disciplinary committee chaired by Mr. William Boakye, our Social studies tutor. The Committee members are well trained to handle both minor and major cases of students.

HEALTH CARE
Health Care is utmost priority in the Institute. The Institute is having Memorandum of Understand with Somanya polyclinic to take good care of our students when they report sick at the polyclinic.

SCHOOL BUS
Somanya Technical Institute has a thirty-three (33) seater bus with registration number GV 222-24. 

2025 Examination Results
!n 2025, the Institute Presented One Hundred and eighty-six (6) candidates. Out of this number the institute had overall score of 97%.
 
INSTITUTIONAL LINKAGES
Ongoing Government Project
As part of Government efforts to transform technical and vocational education in Ghana in providing access, quality and relevance in the TVET sector of Ghana, Government through the GETFUND, is providing three (3) projects to the Institute. The projects include:
Dining Hall
Three storey Girls Dormitory
18- Unit classroom Block

CHALLENGES
Inadequate staffing for both instructional and administrative staff.
Inadequate storage facilities to keep food supplies received from buffer stock
Lack of practical material for effective training
Construction of fence wall

CONCLUSION
Despite the inadequacy of technical and Vocational facilities in the Institute, Somanya Technical Institute continue to produce skilled graduates who excel in their various careers. 


2.2 INSTITUTIONAL PICTURES

column14: Botoku Technical Institute is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution committed to equipping learners with practical skills, knowledge, and competencies for sustainable employment and national development. The institute serves as a key center for technical education in the North Dayi District of the Volta Region of Ghana.
column15:
BOTOKU TECHNICAL INSTITUTE – INSTITUTIONAL PROFILE
1. Introduction
Botoku Technical Institute is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution committed to equipping learners with practical skills, knowledge, and competencies for sustainable employment and national development. The institute serves as a key center for technical education in the North Dayi District of the Volta Region of Ghana.
2. Historical Background
Botoku Technical Institute was originally established in the 1993/1994 academic year as an Integrated Community Centre for Employable Skills (ICCES) under the Social Welfare Department. 

The institution later experienced operational challenges and was revived in the 2016/2017 academic year under the supervision of the TVET authorities.

Since its re-establishment, the institute has continued to provide technical education to students from Botoku and surrounding communities such as Vakpo, Have, Wusuta,Tsrukpe and Tsorxor.

3. Location
Town: Botoku
District: North Dayi District
Region: Volta Region
Country: Ghana
The school is situated in a serene environment conducive for teaching, learning, and skills development.

4. Status and Governance
Ownership: Government of Ghana
Supervising Body: Ghana TVET Service / Ministry of Education
Category: Public Technical Institute
Gender: Mixed (Boys and Girls)
Residential Status: Granted Boarding status for 2026/27 academic year.

5. Vision
To become a centre of excellence in technical and vocational education in Ghana, producing highly skilled, innovative, and self-reliant graduates.

6. Mission
To provide quality, competency-based technical and vocational education and training that meets the needs of industry and contributes to national development. 

7. Core Values
Discipline
Integrity
Excellence
Innovation
Practical Skills Development
Teamwork

8. Academic Programmes
The institute offers a range of technical and vocational programmes aimed at skill acquisition and employability, including:
1.Building Construction Technology
2.Wood Construction Technology
3.Electrical Engineering Technology
4.Fashion Design Technology
5.Hospitality and Catering Management
6.Cosmetology (Hairdressing) 

9. Academic Structure
Competency-Based Training (CBT) approach
Practical-oriented teaching and learning
Continuous assessment and project work
Industrial attachment and internship programmes

10. Facilities
Workshops for practical training (construction, woodwork, electrical)
Fashion and textiles laboratory
Catering and hospitality training unit
Classrooms and administrative block
Open learning spaces.

column14: The Kanda Technical Institute is one of Ghana’s TVET Institutes.  Their mission is to provide demand-driven training and skills to all Ghanaians.  The Institute was established in 1999 under the Ministry of Employment and Social Welfare but currently under the Ghana TVET Service.
column15:
VISION: We deliver TVET that stimulates creativity, innovation and entrepreneurship to meet industry demands for sustainable national development 

MISSION: To be a globally competitive industry driven TVET delivery institute for sustainable national development

VALUES
1. Excellence: We are committed to maintaining high standards of vocational and technical excellence and academic proficiency in all areas of our programs.

2. Innovation: We embrace new technologies, teaching methods, and research to enhance the learning experience and stay ahead of industry trends.

3. Collaboration: We believe that collaboration is the key to success, and we foster an environment where students, stakeholders, faculty, and staff can work together to achieve common goals. This will be much achieved through teamwork, respect for all, truth, honesty, and integrity.

4. Diversity: We celebrate diversity and inclusivity, and we strive to provide an educational environment that is welcoming to people of all backgrounds and cultures.

5. Community: We are committed to serving our local community and creating opportunities for our students to give back through service learning and community outreach programs.
